content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 12, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 12, 2026, focused on identifying critical bottlenecks in speculative decoding for M-RoPE models and advancing the integration of OpenVINO runtimes for Intel hardware. Efforts centered on diagnostic analysis and hardware-specific validation to ensure stable performance across diverse AI agent deployments.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Speculative Decoding Diagnostics:**
    *   Investigated performance degradation in Qwen3.5 models using speculative decoding, identifying a conflict between the M-RoPE position-validation path and the harness's boundary-token re-feed pattern.
    *   Proposed mitigation strategies to prevent silent performance collapse, including upfront compatibility checks for M-RoPE bodies.
*   **Intel Hardware & OpenVINO Integration:**
    *   Advanced the RFC for integrating `openvino` as a `LocalRuntimeKernel`, focusing on leveraging NPU acceleration for ASR tasks.
    *   Validated that while NPU acceleration is highly effective for static-graph encoder workloads like Whisper, it is currently suboptimal for autoregressive LLM text generation.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding Compatibility:**
    *   [#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631): Reported a critical issue where M-RoPE position validation rejects cross-drafter Qwen3.5 batches, causing significant performance drops (below 1 t/s).
*   **Intel Runtime Infrastructure:**
    *   [#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633): Proposed an RFC to add `openvino` (CPU/GPU/NPU) as a `LocalRuntimeKernel` to support Intel hardware acceleration.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **[#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631) Speculative Decoding M-RoPE Validation:**
    *   Extensive discussion regarding the root cause of M-RoPE rejection. Contributors identified that the strict monotone-position requirement in `llama-batch.cpp` conflicts with the spec-decode harness.
    *   A mitigation patch was implemented in the `elizaOS/llama.cpp` fork to prevent silent degeneration, though full support for M-RoPE speculative decoding remains an open challenge requiring upstream changes.
*   **[#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633) OpenVINO RFC:**
    *   The community reached a consensus to narrow the scope of OpenVINO integration to ASR-only tasks, as LLM text generation performance on NPU is currently insufficient.
    *   Hardware probes confirmed that while Intel accelerator nodes are detectable, proper runtime installation remains a prerequisite for full functionality. Future work will focus on recommender and catalog gating to prevent incorrect routing of LLM tasks to OpenVINO.
